summary: PII — training-set update flow with versioned model registry
description_md:
As a compliance-officer, I want to update PII-tagged training datasets through a controlled workflow that maintains versioned snapshots in the model registry, so that I can demonstrate audit-ready provenance for every machine learning model trained on customer data.

## Background

The Compliance Vault module provides PII auto-tagging capabilities that identify sensitive customer attributes across data sources. When training datasets are updated, the model registry must preserve historical versions to support regulatory audits and model reproducibility requirements.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. When a compliance-officer submits a new training dataset, the system automatically applies PII auto-tagging rules from the Compliance Vault and displays detected sensitive fields before ingestion.
2. Upon dataset approval, the system creates a versioned entry in the model registry with metadata including timestamp, source connector, PII categories detected, and retention policy applied.
3. Previous versions of training datasets remain immutable and accessible via the model registry UI with full audit log entries tracking who accessed or exported each version.
4. The compliance-officer can generate an audit report showing all training-set updates with associated model versions, PII tags, and retention status for a selected date range.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Started implementation of versioned model registry integration. PII auto-tagging hook is ready; working on audit trail persistence.
